meaning: vi.默许,纵容,暗中合作,密谋策划
examples: to beat or connive at others to beat a prisoner;
The prisoners paid the guards to connive at their escape.
God cannot alter the past; that is why he is obliged to connive at the existence of historian.
Critics of Chile's higher-education system say that some universities connive at, or even encourage, this ignorance, using a high first-year intake to finance their thinly populated later years.
"It may be that the very wide definition of the word collusion that Judge Cory adopted was due to his concentration on one of the synonyms, namely the verb connive, " the inquiry report said.
